lastReleaseMessage: Security: retire the .finances remote-history finding. The 2026-06-24 repo delete-and-recreate purged the three pre-rewrite commits (43ea16b, f670d17, 44b7b10) that carried real bank statements; gh api now returns 422 for each SHA and 404 for the .finances path. Marks docs/FINANCES_REMOTE_GC.md and the .gitignore exposure note RESOLVED. Also picks up a best-effort usage-dashboard refresh hook in release.ps1.

barRaise.topFinding: The cycle's headline is real movement on the one carried exposure that has dominated the last several reviews: v0.13.5 (1cbdb9e, Jun 24) deleted the three stale dependabot branches that were keeping the pre-rewrite .finances/ commits reachable on the GitHub remote, so 43ea16b, f670d17, and 44b7b10 are now fully unreferenced (git branch -r --contains returns nothing), and it shipped docs/FINANCES_REMOTE_GC.md with two concrete close-out paths. The finding stays MEDIUM only because an unreferenced commit is still served by direct SHA until GitHub garbage-collects it, but the exposure narrowed from reachable-via-branch-refs to orphan-SHA on a private repo and now has a one-command instant fix (delete + recreate the repo from clean local main). The rest of the app path is clean for the eleventh straight cycle: the 2026-06-25 canonical audit confirms 0.13.5 version pills consistent across all four packages plus the DESIGN header, a 90-route API surface with zero drift both directions, and the five base tables mirroring db.ts. The only carried structural risk is the archived keytar on the encrypted-boot path. Verdict holds at healthy.
